overall_summary: Big gap to reference, but the data shows exactly where it is hiding. Attack these points. Current gap: 57.034s slower than reference. The biggest opportunity is Sector 1 (+54.754s), where 55.4 km/h is being left on the table through the corners. These corrections alone are worth 0.59s. That is a significant step forward.

priority_actions.issue: Entry speed is 59.3 km/h below reference but apex speed is acceptable — over-braking on entry.
priority_actions.instruction: Trail the brakes into the corner rather than releasing fully at turn-in. Carry more entry speed and use trail braking to rotate the car to the apex.

sector_feedback.headline: Minimum corner speed is 55.4 km/h below reference — driver is over-slowing significantly through this sector.
sector_feedback.details: Average throttle application is 26% lower than reference — driver is hesitant getting back on power.

sector_feedback.headline: Carrying 4.3 km/h less than reference through corners — small gains available at each apex.
sector_feedback.details: Throttle commitment is strong — 6% higher average than reference.

sector_feedback.headline: Solid sector — 0.960s off reference.
sector_feedback.details: Corner speed is 14.0 km/h above reference — good commitment through the bends.

corner_coaching.fix: Trail the brakes into the corner rather than releasing fully at turn-in. Carry more entry speed and use trail braking to rotate the car to the apex.

all_issues.issue: Braking 180m later than reference — risk of running wide.
all_issues.fix: Bring the brake point back by 180m for consistency. Reference uses 2510m.
